title: `podman image list --format json` does not list `repository`
body:
### Issue Description

`podman image list` seems to be missing important information when adding `--format json` to get structured output, omitting repository and tag information that is present when running without `--format json`.

This is particularly problematic given that `docker image list --format json` _does_ include repository information, so any setup that is currently using docker and is looking to switch to podman can't just drop-in replace docker for podman and instead needs additional code (potentially in _many_ places) just to get what should be its own dedicated field by pulling apart other fields.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue:

1. run `podman image list` and note that the result shows the repository, tag, image id, created datetime, and size:

   ```
   $ podman image list              
   REPOSITORY                   TAG         IMAGE ID      CREATED         SIZE
   localhost/project-093e2ff6   latest      05c078447f2b  7 minutes ago   487 MB
   localhost/pomax-basic-html   latest      a079214e06f0  27 minutes ago  487 MB
   localhost/local-base-image   latest      d569b756a01c  27 hours ago    487 MB
   docker.io/library/alpine     latest      706db57fb206  7 weeks ago     8.62 MB
   ```
 
1. run `podman image list --format json` and note that the result does not list repository or tag.

   ```
   $ podman image list --format json               
   [
       {
           "Id": "05c078447f2bf70e45143f34371cc0b050c080f0a9b17e12021d664c3122b75d",
           "ParentId": "d569b756a01ca0c3dd2b7852f8a066100b0bf798c3841913895d1de8db8e8bfd",
           "RepoTags": null,
           "RepoDigests": [
               "localhost/docker-project-093e2ff6@sha256:a19f49d27aecd21c3e1d493d59a914010fd770ca319527ef0d0d810c85eb80c6"
           ],
           "Size": 486890426,
           "SharedSize": 0,
           "VirtualSize": 486890426,
           "Labels": {
               "io.buildah.version": "1.42.0"
           },
           "Containers": 0,
           "Arch": "amd64",
           "Digest": "sha256:a19f49d27aecd21c3e1d493d59a914010fd770ca319527ef0d0d810c85eb80c6",
           "History": [
               "localhost/docker-project-093e2ff6:latest"
           ],
           "IsManifestList": false,
           "Names": [
               "localhost/docker-project-093e2ff6:latest"
           ],
           "Os": "linux",
           "Created": 1764360400,
           "CreatedAt": "2025-11-28T20:06:40Z"
       },
       {
           "Id": "a079214e06f0483effe87b8390d8f71628a9a03b5a964b26bbd7c0820f23463a",
           "ParentId": "d569b756a01ca0c3dd2b7852f8a066100b0bf798c3841913895d1de8db8e8bfd",
           "RepoTags": null,
           "RepoDigests": [
               "localhost/pomax-basic-html@sha256:f67f75c0beffd290d359106f249bfaddf023e6a0ad4e163836a1cde678ec01ef"
           ],
           "Size": 486890422,

           },

       },
       {
           "Id": "d569b756a01ca0c3dd2b7852f8a066100b0bf798c3841913895d1de8db8e8bfd",
           "ParentId": "bfc76c95152938bb8c3122fb0b30c6cbff9a290e228114407567c4f657d295df",
           "RepoTags": null,
           "RepoDigests": [
               "localhost/local-base-image@sha256:3d45ed4f353f16e6770660c852ecb34a0188fb8585bc1b920132b351798228e4"
           ],
           "Size": 486890221,
           "SharedSize": 0,
           "VirtualSize": 486890221,
           "Labels": {
               "io.buildah.version": "1.42.0"
           },
           "Containers": 0,
           "Arch": "amd64",
           "Digest": "sha256:3d45ed4f353f16e6770660c852ecb34a0188fb8585bc1b920132b351798228e4",
           "History": [

           ],
           "Os": "linux",
           "Created": 1764262140,
           "CreatedAt": "2025-11-27T16:49:00Z"
       },
       {
           "Id": "706db57fb2063f39f69632c5b5c9c439633fda35110e65587c5d85553fd1cc38",
           "ParentId": "",
           "RepoTags": null,
           "RepoDigests": [
               "docker.io/library/alpine@sha256:4b7ce07002c69e8f3d704a9c5d6fd3053be500b7f1c69fc0d80990c2ad8dd412",
               "docker.io/library/alpine@sha256:85f2b723e106c34644cd5851d7e81ee87da98ac54672b29947c052a45d31dc2f"
           ],
           "Size": 8619075,
           "SharedSize": 0,
           "VirtualSize": 8619075,
           "Labels": null,
           "Containers": 0,
           "Arch": "amd64",
           "Digest": "sha256:4b7ce07002c69e8f3d704a9c5d6fd3053be500b7f1c69fc0d80990c2ad8dd412",
           "History": [
               "docker.io/library/alpine:latest"
           ],
           "IsManifestList": false,
           "Names": [
               "docker.io/library/alpine:latest"
           ],
           "Os": "linux",
           "Created": 1759921496,
           "CreatedAt": "2025-10-08T11:04:56Z"
       }
   ]
   ```
 
1. run `docker image list --format json` and note that that result _does_ include the repository (even if it lies about it being JSON - it's only JSON "per result" so if there's more than one result, each line is its own JSON string, instead of the full output being valid JSON using array syntax):

   ```
   {"Containers":"N/A","CreatedAt":"2025-08-27 17:31:50 +0000 UTC","CreatedSince":"3 months ago","Digest":"\u003cnone\u003e","ID":"7fb3ba88cd9d","Repository":"local-base-image","SharedSize":"N/A","Size":"485MB","Tag":"latest","UniqueSize":"N/A","VirtualSize":"484.9MB"}
   ```

### Describe the results you received

See above

### Describe the results you expected

I expect podman to at the very least show the same fields as Docker, so that scripts and code that was written for docker doesn't break when swapping over to podman.

comments.body: Also an issue on Fedora Linux it seems

This seems like a pretty easy fix: the [`writeJSON` function](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/cmd/podman/images/list.go#L161) is missing these fields. Just a couple lines and it's working for me locally.

I am a little confused about the presence of the `RepoTags` field; afaict it is always `null`.

~Someone more versed than me may also know if this needs to be fixed for `podman machine list --format json` as well.~ EDIT: This was a stupid question, the `machine` command would have no use for these fields.

Ooh, nice find!

Yeah it's a bit odd to have an "always nil" field, but as long as the fields include "the same, and then more" fields as Docker, that's pretty much all folks need to drop-in replace docker with podman =)
I have a feeling there's a reason, but I couldn't find anything specific. WDYT? @mheon @Luap99

If there was a reason it doesn't seem to be documented in the git history. 

I see no issues with adding the two fields in the json output. Looking at the code we even have them already as part of imageReporter, it is just that the writeJson() function throws them away again by embedding only entities.ImageSummary instead of imageReporter. And if we do that then I think the nil on RepoTags can make sense as there would be no reason to print both Repositrory/Tag and RepoTags.
